First of all, I’m pretty much aware that there are lots of bad reviews out there. I’m sure that you’ve seen them as well. The thing is, “X-Men: Dark Phoenix” is not as bad as what critics are saying. In my opinion, this film is better than “X-Men: Apocalypse” and the previous Phoenix Saga adaptation, “X-Men: The Last Stand”.

”X-Men: Dark Phoenix” clearly shows that Sophie Turner is strong enough to carry this film, she’s amazing. Even though the script for the film is a bit generic, not to mention that there are some cringeworthy ones. The first and the second act of the film are a bit slow, however, things are getting 180° different in the third act. By different, I mean in a good way. The entire soundtrack of the film is excellent, as the entire score is composed by Hans Zimmer.

From the recurring cast, James McAvoy’s Professor X and Michael Fassbender’s Magneto got their awesome chemistry as usual. Jennifer Lawrence and Nicholas Hoult are also amazing. I can finally feel the chemistry of Tye Sheridan’s Scott with Sophie Turner’s Jean. It’s also nice knowing that Kodi Smit-McPhee’s Nightcrawler and Alexandra Shipp’s Storm finally deserve more spotlight. The newcomer Jessica Chastain, she’s good but her villain role is weak, and I’m not talking about her powers. It’s just I can’t feel her role as a villain. Here’s an advice, keep your eyes sharp to see some cameos of X-Men’s familiar mutants from the comics.

In the end, despite what the critics are saying, “X-Men: Dark Phoenix” is still quite enjoyable and entertaining. A decent way to end FOX’s X-Men franchise, kinda.

7/10

If you’re wondering for a credit scene, the answer is no, there are no mid or post credit scene for this film.

I'm confusee,,  anyone please explain to me about the timeline

X-Men 1-3 and the first two Wolverine films: Old Timeline

X-Men: Future Past creates a new Timeline due to time travel. Anything after this: New and current Timeline. 

This movie is set in the 90s, thus Professor X is played by McAvoy while in Logan he is played by Patrick Stewart. He is still alive due to the events in Future Past. 

Deadpool is a bit more complicated, especially DP2 where you can't say if the time travel shenanigans played for laughs is canon or not. They don't connect back to the main plot, so they are treated as an own spin-off Timeline by fans.

Hard to watch this without judging it in light of the classic Claremont/Byrne/Austin storyline; I'm not a huge X-Men fan, really, but that story was just so well-written and so visually stunning that any adaptation of it is going to have to contend with it, at least in the realm of my own imagination and, I am guessing, in the imaginations of many of those who read it. Is this possibly inspired by a later version of the Phoenix story, such as from the Ultimate universe? The actors all look like they are doing a great job (the Jean Grey actress looks very sinister and intimidating in the portions where she is intended to), but when I see the title Dark Phoenix, it makes me want to see some of the wild, imaginative imagery of Byrne and Austin on the big screen.

im not. it got played out after the first time. in DoFP it was brilliant and unexpected. in Apocalypse it was formulaic and as though Fox catered the plot just to accommodate that scene. the fact we all probably guess they'll do the same trick again says a lot.

Dark Phoenix, one of my fave comic story archs. It really solidified my distain for Professor X. He caged her mind 'cos he was afraid she couldn't control her power, or that she would use it against them. Not allowing her the oppourtunity to truely master it or choose, is unforgivable. Burn the bloody place down, Phoenix!
There are some things that can't be controlled. Especially a force outside yourself.

As far as this particular portrayal of the Phoenix Force goes, as a separate entity all its own, it could not be "mastered" or "controlled" by anyone. It only joined with Jean because it felt a sort of connection to her, but it ultimately only wants the chaos of destruction and creation, and if it felt Jean's will was trying to overpower or control it, it would likely retaliate. Even in the original story line, before the Phoenix Force was retconned into a separate entity, and was purely the full realization of Jean's power, she couldn't control it and it drove her mad.

one thought, one goal... survive... Survive and one day return home. The

shipwrecked but the man who will bring justice to those who have 
poisoned my city. My name is Oliver Queen

Season 1 Ep. 2

Day
 I went missing, was the day I died. Five years in hell forged me into a

his life for mine. In his final moments, he told me that truth... That 
our family's wealth had been built on the suffering of others. That he 
failed our city, and that it was up to me to save it and right his 
wrongs. But to do that without endangering the people closest to me, I 
have to be someone else. I have to be something else.
